title: Digital Footprint Audit
prerequisites: Access to WHOIS, dig, Shodan (free tier)
learning_objectives: Perform structured passive reconnaissance using OSINT techniques
Enumerate domain, personnel, and technology information without active scanning
Produce a professional passive recon report
key_topics: DNS record analysis (A, MX, NS, TXT, CNAME)
scenario: An NGO in Gaborone is about to launch a new donor portal. Before going live, they have hired you to assess their digital footprint using only passive techniques. They want to know what an attacker could learn without touching their systems.

scenario_context.setting: NGO in Gaborone preparing to launch a donor portal
scenario_context.challenge: Complete passive reconnaissance with zero active scanning
scenario_context.relevance: Many African organisations have significant digital footprints they are unaware of. Donor portals handle sensitive financial data and are attractive targets.

subtasks.title: Domain Intelligence
subtasks.description: Perform WHOIS and DNS enumeration on the provided domain. Document registrar info, nameservers, MX records, and subdomains.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Run whois on the domain. Use dig for A, MX, NS, TXT, CNAME records. Check for subdomains using passive tools like crt.sh. Document everything with timestamps.
subtasks.evidence_required: WHOIS results, DNS records table, subdomain list

subtasks.title: People & Email Discovery
subtasks.description: Using OSINT, find employee emails, LinkedIn profiles, and organisational structure data.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Search LinkedIn for the organisation. Note employee names, roles, email patterns. Check Have I Been Pwned for domain breaches. Use tools like theHarvester if available.
subtasks.evidence_required: Email list, LinkedIn summaries, org structure notes

subtasks.title: Technology Stack Fingerprinting
subtasks.description: Without touching the target, identify web technologies, CMS, hosting provider, and visible technology stack.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use Wappalyzer, BuiltWith, or Netcraft. Check HTTP headers visible in cached versions. Identify CDN, CMS, frameworks, hosting provider. Check Shodan for any exposed services.

subtasks.title: Exposure Report
subtasks.description: Compile findings into a structured Passive Recon Report with executive summary, methodology, findings, and risk ratings.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Structure: Executive Summary, Methodology, Domain Intelligence, Personnel Exposure, Technology Stack, Risk Assessment, Recommendations. Rate each finding by risk level.

reflection_prompt: You discover that the NGO's donor database has been exposed in a previous breach. How do you communicate this to the client responsibly, and what immediate actions do you recommend?

quiz.question: Passive reconnaissance means:
options: Gathering information without directly interacting with the target
quiz.explanation: Passive recon gathers information from public sources without sending any traffic to the target systems.

quiz.question: WHOIS data is useful because:
options: It shows domain registration details, ownership, and contact information
quiz.explanation: WHOIS provides registration details that can reveal ownership, hosting, and organisational information.

mentor_notes: Evaluate thoroughness of OSINT coverage. Strong submissions will go beyond basic WHOIS and find subdomains, email patterns, breach data. Report quality matters — this is a client deliverable.
